message:
feat(server): move JSON-RPC dispatch to spawn_blocking, force epoll reactor for ATC operator

Three significant changes to the HTTP server runtime:

1. JSON-RPC dispatch moved to spawn_blocking:
   - dispatch() becomes async, uses a self_ref: OnceLock<Weak<HttpState>>
     to upgrade to an Arc and delegate synchronous dispatch_inner work
     to asupersync::runtime::spawn_blocking
   - Keeps all router/DB work off async worker threads, preventing
     blocking operations from starving the event loop
   - handle() loses #[allow(clippy::unused_async)] (now genuinely async)
   - push_event replaced with push_event_async().await

2. ATC operator forced to epoll reactor:
   - Explicitly constructs an EpollReactor on Linux (#[cfg(not(target_os
     = "linux"))] fallback) instead of relying on the default reactor
   - Avoids io_uring hangs in handle_reserve_ticket D-state that were
     observed under high concurrency

3. HTTP runtime: log worker count and configure blocking_threads:
   - build_http_runtime() stores worker thread count, logs it alongside
     reactor name, passes .blocking_threads(workers, 64) to RuntimeBuilder

Dependencies (Cargo.lock):
- Deduplicate asupersync: remove git-sourced duplicate (rev 82cee07)
  and its duplicate franken-* sub-crate entries
- All workspace crates now resolve to single local asupersync
- Add io-uring as new dependency of local asupersync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
